content.title: Agentic AI Architecture and Multi-Agent Collaboration
content.description: How multiple AI agents collaborate on complex tasks
content.markdown:
# Agentic AI Architecture and Multi-Agent Collaboration

## What is Agentic AI?

Agentic AI is an emerging discipline in which **multiple AI agents collaborate** on complex tasks. Rather than a single AI model processing a request, specialized agents work together - each bringing distinct capabilities, tools, and expertise to the problem.

## MeshWeaver's Multi-Agent Framework

MeshWeaver provides the orchestration layer for agentic conversations, enabling:

- **Agent Definition**: Configure agents with specific roles, tools, and model assignments
- **Tool Access**: Each agent can invoke tools within MeshWeaver and external systems
- **Conversation Routing**: Direct subtasks to the most appropriate agent
- **Context Sharing**: Pass relevant data between agents via the addressable data mesh

## Agent Specialization Pattern

| Agent Type | Role | Recommended Model | Rationale |
|------------|------|-------------------|----------|
| **Planning Agent** | Develops task plans, coordinates subtasks | GPT Reasoning / Claude Opus | Complex reasoning, multi-step planning |
| **Research Agent** | Searches across data mesh, gathers context | GPT mini / Claude Haiku | Fast, cost-effective for retrieval |
| **Extraction Agent** | Extracts structured data from documents | Azure Document AI + LLM | Specialized for document parsing |
| **Classification Agent** | Categorizes documents, assigns partners | GPT / Claude Sonnet | Pattern matching, consistent outputs |
| **Triage Agent** | Prioritizes emails, assesses urgency | GPT mini / Claude Haiku | Fast processing of high volumes |

## Cost Optimization Through Model Mapping

By matching model capability to task complexity, MeshWeaver optimizes both cost and performance.

## Tool Access Framework

### Internal Tools
- **Data Mesh Query**: Access any addressable data in the mesh
- **Document Parser**: Extract content from PDFs, Excel, Word
- **Vector Search**: Semantic search across indexed content
- **Business Rule Engine**: Apply configured validation rules
- **Workflow Trigger**: Initiate downstream processes

### External Tools
- **Document Repository APIs**: Query and update document management systems
- **Outlook Connector**: Read emails, send drafts
- **Azure Cognitive Services**: OCR, form recognition
- **Custom APIs**: Any REST endpoint can be configured as a tool

## Architecture Benefits

### Resilience
- Individual agent failures don't crash the entire workflow
- Retry logic at the agent level
- Fallback to alternative models if primary is unavailable

### Observability
- Full trace of agent interactions
- Token usage and cost tracking per agent
- Performance metrics for optimization

### Extensibility
- Add new agents without changing existing workflows
- Plug in new models as they become available
- Custom tools can be added via REST API configuration

### Governance
- Control which agents access which data
- Audit trail of all AI decisions
- Human-in-the-loop checkpoints configurable per workflow
